## Refreshing Rose Badger Game Implementation

### Game Description

Refreshing Rose Badger is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a 5x5 square board. Players take turns placing their pieces on the board. The goal is to create a line of five pieces in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

### Simplified Version

Here's a simplified version of the game implemented in Ludax:

```ludax
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Incremental Addition of Rules

Let's add some rules to make the game more interesting. We'll introduce a "rose" piece that can capture an opponent's piece.

```ludax
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty) 
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Further Addition of Rules

Now, let's add a "badger" piece that can move an opponent's piece.

```ludax
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty) 
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                        (move (adjacent occupied) destination:empty)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Complete Game

Here's the complete game with all the rules:

```ludax
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (center))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty) 
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                        (move (adjacent occupied) destination:empty)
                    )
                    (result 
                        (not (line 5))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

This implementation provides a basic structure for the Refreshing Rose Badger game. You can modify and extend the rules to create a more complex and engaging game.

Refreshing Rose Badger Game Implementation
=====================================

### Game Description

Refreshing Rose Badger is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board with a size of 7x7. Players take turns placing their pieces on empty squares. The goal is to create a line of five pieces in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

### Game Rules

#### Equipment

The game is played on a 7x7 square board.

#### Start Rules

The game starts with an empty board.

#### Play Rules

1.  Players alternate turns.
2.  On each turn, a player places one piece on an empty square.
3.  A player cannot place a piece on a square that is already occupied.

#### End Rules

The game ends when:

*   A player creates a line of five pieces in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally). That player wins.
*   The board is full, and no player has won. The game is a draw.

### Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

### Incremental Rule Additions

#### Adding a Start Rule

To add a start rule that places a piece on the center square for one of the players:

```ludax
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

#### Adding a Capture Effect

To add a capture effect that removes an opponent's piece when a player places a piece adjacent to it:

```ludax
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

#### Adding a Forced Pass

To add a forced pass rule that forces a player to pass if they have no valid moves:

```ludax
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 7))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )

        (end
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

## Refreshing Rose Badger Game Implementation

### Game Description

Refreshing Rose Badger is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board with a size of 7x7. Players take turns placing their pieces on the board. The goal is to create a line of five pieces in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

### Game Rules

#### Equipment

The game is played on a 7x7 square board.

#### Start Rules

The game starts with an empty board.

#### Play Rules

1. Players take turns placing one piece on the board per turn.
2. A player can only place a piece on an empty square.
3. A player cannot place a piece on a square that is already occupied.

#### End Rules

The game ends when one player creates a line of five pieces in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally). That player wins the game. If all squares on the board are filled and no player has won, the game is a draw.

### Ludax Implementation

```lark
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Incremental Rule Additions

#### Rule 1: Capturing Pieces

Players can capture their opponent's pieces by surrounding them.

```lark
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty) 
                       (effects 
                           (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                       )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

#### Rule 2: Scoring

Players earn points for capturing their opponent's pieces.

```lark
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty) 
                       (effects 
                           (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                           (increment_score mover 1)
                       )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Future Enhancements

* Implement a more complex winning condition, such as creating a specific pattern on the board.
* Add more pieces with different abilities.
* Introduce obstacles or power-ups on the board.
* Create a time limit for each player's turn.

## Refreshing Rose Badger Game Implementation

### Game Description

Refreshing Rose Badger is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board with a size of 7x7. The objective of the game is to create a line of five or more pieces in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

### Game Rules

#### Equipment

The game is played on a 7x7 square board.

#### Start Rules

The game starts with an empty board. Player 1 places their piece in the center of the board.

#### Play Rules

Players alternate turns. A player can place their piece on an empty square.

#### End Rules

The game ends when a player creates a line of five or more pieces in any direction. The game also ends when the board is full, resulting in a draw.

### Ludax Implementation

```lark
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)

        (board (square 7))
    )

            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
```
```lark
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

#### Scoring

To add scoring, we can modify the end rules to include a by_score result.

```lark
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
```

```ludax
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5 exact:true) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

## Refreshing Rose Badger Game Implementation

### Game Description

Refreshing Rose Badger is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a 5x5 square board. Players take turns placing their pieces on the board, trying to create lines of five pieces in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally.

### Initial Implementation

Here is the initial implementation of the Refreshing Rose Badger game in Ludax:

```ludax
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Start Rules

Let's add a start rule to place the first piece on the center of the board.

```ludax
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Effects

Let's add an effect to capture opponent pieces when a player creates a line of five pieces.

```ludax
(game "Refreshing Rose Badger"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (line 4) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

```ludax
                (place

                    (effects

                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )

    (rendering

        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

This implementation provides a basic structure for the Refreshing Rose Badger game. You can further extend and modify the rules to create a more complex and engaging game.
